explanations.12: array_rand() picks a random index from the array, so $words[array_rand($words)] gets a random word from the list.
explanations.26: We're using the incorrect guess count as an index to pick which hangman drawing to show—0 guesses wrong shows the first drawing, 1 shows the second, and so on.
explanations.33: str_split() breaks a string into an array of individual characters, so we can check each letter one by one.
explanations.40: trim() removes the extra space from the end of the string we built by adding spaces after each letter or underscore.
explanations.54: fgets(STDIN) reads what the user types from the keyboard and trim() removes the newline character at the end.
explanations.56: strlen($guess) !== 1 checks that exactly one character was entered, and !ctype_alpha($guess) checks that it's actually a letter.
explanations.70: strpos() looks for the guessed letter in the word; if it returns false (meaning not found), we subtract from incorrect guesses.
explanations.74: This line has a logic problem—displayWord() echoes output instead of returning a string, so comparing it won't work as intended to detect a win.
